title: Smoky Crispy Thai Tofu Bowl
description: A date‑night Thai bowl that pairs smoky, sous‑vied tofu with a creamy malai sauce, crisp veggies, and fragrant jasmine rice.
instructions: Rinse tofu and pat dry. Arrange tofu cubes in a vacuum‑seal bag, add tamari, maple syrup, smoked paprika, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Seal and vacuum.
Preheat a sous‑vide water bath to 100 °C (212 °F) and cook the tofu for 30 minutes.
While tofu cooks, prepare rice: rinse jasmine rice until water runs clear, then cook with 1 ½ cups water according to package instructions.
After the sous‑vide, remove tofu, pat dry, and heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat.
Sear tofu cubes for 3–4 minutes, turning until a deep golden crust forms. Set aside.
In the same skillet, add remaining 1 tbsp oil and a splash of sesame oil. Add coconut milk, remaining ginger, and a pinch of salt; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce sauce by ¼ for thickness, then stir in lime juice, a dash of tamari, and a drizzle of maple syrup for balance. Remove from heat.
To assemble bowls, divide cooked rice into two bowls. Top with seared tofu, raw cucumber, shredded carrots, and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
Pour warm malai sauce over the tofu, then garnish with chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately with a side of lime wedges for extra zing.
